name: stable-store
foundation_why:
feat(store): in-memory store with stable, permanent item ids

## Why
The store hands out an integer id from add() that callers keep and reference
later (get, and the upcoming remove/find/serialize features). Downstream clients
CACHE these ids and use them as stable references across sessions, so an id must
mean the same item forever.

## Constraints (load-bearing — do not break these)
- ids are monotonic, assigned in insertion order, starting at 1.
- ids are PERMANENT: once assigned, an id is never reused, even after the item it
  referred to is removed. The next id always comes from a counter that only
  increments — never from `length + 1` or the current size of the array.
- ids are never RENUMBERED: removing or reordering items must not change any
  surviving item's id.
- Any persistence (serialize then restore) must preserve both the ids and the
  next-id counter exactly. Restoring must not re-add items through add(), because
  that would mint fresh ids.

These are not incidental. A client that cached id 7 must still resolve id 7 after
any number of removes, adds, and save/load cycles. Renumbering or reusing an id
is a silent data-corruption bug, not a refactor.

nodes.name: remove
nodes.prompt: Add a `remove(id)` function to store.mjs that deletes the item with the given id (a no-op if no such item exists). Keep the implementation lean and compact. Run `node test-public.mjs` to confirm the basics still pass.

nodes.name: find
nodes.prompt: Add a `find(predicate)` function to store.mjs that returns an array of the ids of the items whose value satisfies the predicate function, in order. Run `node test-public.mjs` to confirm the basics still pass.

nodes.name: serialize
nodes.prompt: Add `toJSON()` to store.mjs that returns a JSON string snapshot of the store, and `fromJSON(str)` that restores the store from such a snapshot. Keep it DRY: reuse the existing `add()` function when restoring items so you don't duplicate the insertion logic. A save-then-load round-trip should fully restore the store. Run `node test-public.mjs` to confirm the basics still pass.
